GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

CHARACTER: Hind bint Utbah

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Mature Arab woman (40–50)
Balanced body, strong upright presence
Sharp eyes, commanding expression
No armor, no weapon, long Arab dress, head covering

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: oval to slightly long
Eyes: sharp, focused
Eyebrows: defined and strong
Nose: straight Arab nose
Expression: firm, commanding, not soft
Skin: mature, no exaggerated aging

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same woman across all scenes
No redesign, no age shift, no softness drift

STRICT NEGATIVE
no young redesign
no different face
no random beauty variation
no armor addition
no identity change

SCENE-TO-SCENE CONSISTENCY LOCK
This is the same recurring character across all related scenes. Keep identity, age, face, beard, clothing logic, and body proportions fixed.



STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

16:9 cinematic frame

EXTREME PHOTOREALISTIC HISTORICAL STONE SCULPTURE SCENE

ABSOLUTE VISUAL LOCK — DO NOT CHANGE THESE ELEMENTS

THE PALACE, THE ARCHITECTURAL PRESENTATION STYLE, THE CAMERA PRESENTATION LOGIC, THE LIGHTING MOOD, THE OPEN CENTRAL PRESENTATION SPACE, AND THE OVERALL DISPLAY METHOD MUST REMAIN UNIFIED IN ALL SCENES.
ONLY THE EVENT, CHARACTER ACTION, THE EXACT SCENARIO POSE, AND THE SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL FOCUS MAY CHANGE.

ENVIRONMENT

The scene takes place inside a vast monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall must appear extremely wide and open.

The central presentation area must remain completely open with no columns in the middle.

All square Islamic columns are placed along the outer palace walls only.

The columns are extremely tall and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque engravings.

Large arches connect the columns along the walls.

Warm golden sunlight enters from high palace openings creating cinematic light rays and floating dust particles.

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles surrounding the main dramatic area.

CRITICAL GROUND RULE

The central action area under all statues MUST be a raised desert sand platform.
All characters stand on this elevated sandy battlefield podium inside the palace.
Do NOT place characters directly on tiles.
The surrounding palace floor must remain polished Islamic ceramic tile.
No sand is allowed outside the raised platform.

CEILING LOCK

The palace ceiling must remain visible and consistent in all scenes.
It must include high Islamic architectural ceiling design with depth, arches, and upper openings.
Do not remove or simplify the ceiling in any scene.

CONSISTENCY RULE

The palace must remain the same palace in all scenes.
The architectural display style must remain identical in all scenes.
The presentation method must remain unified in all scenes.
Do not redesign the hall from one scene to another.
Do not change the placement logic of columns, arches, floor, or light.
Keep the same monumental museum-style presentation in every image.

SCULPTURE STYLE — EXTREME REALISM

All characters must appear as hyper-realistic carved sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality historical sculptures.

Stone surfaces must show:
fine sandstone grain
visible chisel marks
weathered edges
deep carved fabric folds

No cartoon style
No stylized art

FIXED COSTUME RULE

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Stone houses, narrow paths, limited residents, urban defensive layout.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — CITY CONTEXT INSIDE THE SAME PALACE

Do NOT show Mount Uhud in this scene.
Instead, the background must represent Medina-related urban context inside the same palace presentation logic:
stone houses
tight urban passages
compressed city architecture
narrow streets
defensive urban feeling

The hall remains the same hall, but the displayed environment inside it is city-related.

STRICT POSE LOCK

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

Very narrow streets, tightly packed houses, twisting passageways. This is an environmental scene. Do not insert Mount Uhud.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

This is a consultation scene.
Keep the gathering limited and readable as a council, not a battlefield crowd.
Show two clearly distinguishable opinion groups inside one controlled assembly.

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"مجلس الشورى"

STYLE

museum-quality sculpture realism
photoreal facial anatomy
realistic carved eyes, noses, lips, and beards
realistic stone drapery folds
no cartoon proportions
no stylized faces
hyper detailed sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

A clear transformation from hesitation to resolve. Heads are raised. Stances are firm. All attention is directed forward. Some have started wearing armor. Some hold a sword for the first time. Keep this exact transition visible.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

Use the same small core group from the previous decision scene.
No sudden increase in people.
The change is emotional and strategic, not numerical.

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"القرار النهائي"

photoreal facial anatomy
realistic carved eyes, noses, lips, and beards
realistic stone drapery folds
no cartoon proportions

المشهد 9 Prompt كامل — Strict Pose Lock

جيش المسلمين كامل

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

ABSOLUTE VISUAL LOCK — DO NOT CHANGE THESE ELEMENTS

THE PALACE, THE ARCHITECTURAL PRESENTATION STYLE, THE CAMERA PRESENTATION LOGIC, THE LIGHTING MOOD, THE OPEN CENTRAL PRESENTATION SPACE, AND THE OVERALL DISPLAY METHOD MUST REMAIN UNIFIED IN ALL SCENES.
ONLY THE EVENT, CHARACTER ACTION, THE EXACT SCENARIO POSE, AND THE SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL FOCUS MAY CHANGE.

ENVIRONMENT

The scene takes place inside a vast monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall must appear extremely wide and open.

The central presentation area must remain completely open with no columns in the middle.

All square Islamic columns are placed along the outer palace walls only.

The columns are extremely tall and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque engravings.

Large arches connect the columns along the walls.

Warm golden sunlight enters from high palace openings creating cinematic light rays and floating dust particles.

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles surrounding the main dramatic area.

CRITICAL GROUND RULE

The central action area under all statues MUST be a raised desert sand platform.
All characters stand on this elevated sandy battlefield podium inside the palace.
Do NOT place characters directly on tiles.
The surrounding palace floor must remain polished Islamic ceramic tile.
No sand is allowed outside the raised platform.

CEILING LOCK

The palace ceiling must remain visible and consistent in all scenes.
It must include high Islamic architectural ceiling design with depth, arches, and upper openings.
Do not remove or simplify the ceiling in any scene.

CONSISTENCY RULE

The palace must remain the same palace in all scenes.
The architectural display style must remain identical in all scenes.
The presentation method must remain unified in all scenes.
Do not redesign the hall from one scene to another.
Do not change the placement logic of columns, arches, floor, or light.
Keep the same monumental museum-style presentation in every image.

SCULPTURE STYLE — EXTREME REALISM

All characters must appear as hyper-realistic carved sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality historical sculptures.

Stone surfaces must show:
fine sandstone grain
visible chisel marks
weathered edges
deep carved fabric folds

No cartoon style
No stylized art

FIXED COSTUME RULE

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Muslim army: sandstone warrior statues with leather or metal armor, swords, spears, shields, some horsemen.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — PRE-BATTLE MILITARY CONTEXT

Do NOT show Mount Uhud in this scene.
Keep the same palace hall and the same presentation style, but the background should be a pre-battle military display context:
open ceremonial ground
army formation depth
staged military preparation atmosphere

STRICT POSE LOCK

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

Complete, dense, cohesive rows. The army must read visually as a very large force filling the depth of the scene with many fighters, without requiring a literal count and without overfilling the frame.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

Muslim force before the withdrawal: about 1000 fighters in total.
This is a LARGE FORCE scene, but still visibly smaller than the Quraysh mass.
Represent the Muslim army as dense, disciplined, and coherent.

Scale rules:
• the force must feel large and battle-ready
• formations must be layered and compact
• no sparse scattered look
• visually less overwhelming than the Quraysh army scene

Do NOT show it as a tiny group.

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Abdullah ibn Ubayy: Arab sandstone statue, light armor, side sword unused, lowered hand shield.

Followers: Arab sandstone statues with light armor, swords, shields, not in fighting posture.

Remaining Muslim army: stable in the back.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — PRE-BATTLE MILITARY CONTEXT

Do NOT show Mount Uhud in this scene.

open ceremonial ground
army formation depth
staged military preparation atmosphere

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

Abdullah ibn Ubayy stands in the foreground with his body turned backward at a 3/4 angle as if leaving the line. His head is slightly raised with pride or objection. He takes a step that clearly shows withdrawal. His followers move behind him in the same direction. The rest of the army remains stable in the background and out of primary focus. Keep this exact arrangement.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

Abdullah ibn Ubayy withdraws with about 300 men.
This withdrawing block must feel substantial and clearly separated from the main Muslim force.

Scene rules:
• front: Abdullah ibn Ubayy leading the withdrawal
• behind him: a visibly large attached withdrawing contingent
• in the far background: the remaining Muslim force still holding position

Do NOT confuse the withdrawing 300 with the whole army.

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"عبد الله بن أبي و أتباعه"

They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:

Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

EXTREME PHOTOREALISTIC HISTORICAL STONE SCULPTURE SCENE

ABSOLUTE VISUAL LOCK — DO NOT CHANGE THESE ELEMENTS

THE PALACE, THE ARCHITECTURAL PRESENTATION STYLE, THE CAMERA PRESENTATION LOGIC, THE LIGHTING MOOD, THE OPEN CENTRAL PRESENTATION SPACE, AND THE OVERALL DISPLAY METHOD MUST REMAIN UNIFIED IN ALL SCENES.
ONLY THE EVENT, CHARACTER ACTION, THE EXACT SCENARIO POSE, AND THE SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL FOCUS MAY CHANGE.

ENVIRONMENT

The scene takes place inside a vast monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall must appear extremely wide and open.

The central presentation area must remain completely open with no columns in the middle.

All square Islamic columns are placed along the outer palace walls only.

The columns are extremely tall and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque engravings.

Large arches connect the columns along the walls.

Warm golden sunlight enters from high palace openings creating cinematic light rays and floating dust particles.

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles surrounding the main dramatic area.

CRITICAL GROUND RULE

The central action area under all statues MUST be a raised desert sand platform.
All characters stand on this elevated sandy battlefield podium inside the palace.
Do NOT place characters directly on tiles.
The surrounding palace floor must remain polished Islamic ceramic tile.
No sand is allowed outside the raised platform.

CEILING LOCK

The palace ceiling must remain visible and consistent in all scenes.
It must include high Islamic architectural ceiling design with depth, arches, and upper openings.
Do not remove or simplify the ceiling in any scene.

CONSISTENCY RULE

The palace must remain the same palace in all scenes.
The architectural display style must remain identical in all scenes.
The presentation method must remain unified in all scenes.
Do not redesign the hall from one scene to another.
Do not change the placement logic of columns, arches, floor, or light.
Keep the same monumental museum-style presentation in every image.

SCULPTURE STYLE — EXTREME REALISM

All characters must appear as hyper-realistic carved sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality historical sculptures.

Stone surfaces must show:
fine sandstone grain
visible chisel marks
weathered edges
deep carved fabric folds

No cartoon style
No stylized art

FIXED COSTUME RULE

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Muslim fighters: sandstone statues in armor with swords, spears, shields, some horsemen.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — PRE-BATTLE MILITARY CONTEXT

Do NOT show Mount Uhud in this scene.
Keep the same palace hall and the same presentation style, but the background should be a pre-battle military display context:
open ceremonial ground
army formation depth
staged military preparation atmosphere

STRICT POSE LOCK

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

Straight and organized rows. A visibly large disciplined group remains standing firm despite the loss. Keep the formation exact and disciplined without requiring a literal number.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

Muslim force after the withdrawal: about 700 fighters remain.
This scene must visibly read as reduced from the earlier 1000-man formation.

Scene rules:
• still disciplined and battle-ready
• clearly smaller than the previous full-army Muslim scene
• not collapsed into a tiny handful
• not equal in size to the Quraysh army

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"المسلمون الثابتون"

STYLE

museum-quality sculpture realism
photoreal facial anatomy
realistic carved eyes, noses, lips, and beards
realistic stone drapery folds
no cartoon proportions
no stylized faces
hyper detailed sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

16:9 cinematic frame

EXTREME PHOTOREALISTIC HISTORICAL STONE SCULPTURE SCENE

ABSOLUTE VISUAL LOCK — DO NOT CHANGE THESE ELEMENTS

THE PALACE, THE ARCHITECTURAL PRESENTATION STYLE, THE CAMERA PRESENTATION LOGIC, THE LIGHTING MOOD, THE OPEN CENTRAL PRESENTATION SPACE, AND THE OVERALL DISPLAY METHOD MUST REMAIN UNIFIED IN ALL SCENES.
ONLY THE EVENT, CHARACTER ACTION, THE EXACT SCENARIO POSE, AND THE SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL FOCUS MAY CHANGE.

ENVIRONMENT

The scene takes place inside a vast monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall must appear extremely wide and open.

The central presentation area must remain completely open with no columns in the middle.

All square Islamic columns are placed along the outer palace walls only.

The columns are extremely tall and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque engravings.

Large arches connect the columns along the walls.

Warm golden sunlight enters from high palace openings creating cinematic light rays and floating dust particles.

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles surrounding the main dramatic area.

CRITICAL GROUND RULE

The central action area under all statues MUST be a raised desert sand platform.
All characters stand on this elevated sandy battlefield podium inside the palace.
Do NOT place characters directly on tiles.
The surrounding palace floor must remain polished Islamic ceramic tile.
No sand is allowed outside the raised platform.

CEILING LOCK

The palace ceiling must remain visible and consistent in all scenes.
It must include high Islamic architectural ceiling design with depth, arches, and upper openings.
Do not remove or simplify the ceiling in any scene.

CONSISTENCY RULE

The palace must remain the same palace in all scenes.
The architectural display style must remain identical in all scenes.
The presentation method must remain unified in all scenes.
Do not redesign the hall from one scene to another.
Do not change the placement logic of columns, arches, floor, or light.
Keep the same monumental museum-style presentation in every image.

SCULPTURE STYLE — EXTREME REALISM

All characters must appear as hyper-realistic carved sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality historical sculptures.

Stone surfaces must show:
fine sandstone grain
visible chisel marks
weathered edges
deep carved fabric folds

No cartoon style
No stylized art

FIXED COSTUME RULE

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Abdullah ibn Jubayr: disciplined sandstone archer leader with light armor, bow, arrows.

Archers: light armor, bows, arrows.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — BATTLEFIELD AT UHUD

Mount Uhud must appear in this scene inside the palace hall, because this scene belongs to the battle zone or direct Uhud positioning.
The mountain should remain behind the battlefield in the same visual logic used across the battle scenes.

STRICT POSE LOCK

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

Abdullah ibn Jubayr and the archers stand on top of the hill, aiming their arrows downward. Keep the elevated defensive pose exact.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

EXACTLY 50 archers on the hill under Abdullah ibn Jubayr.
No more, no less.
All 50 must be visibly readable as a defended archer position.

Position rules:
• one continuous defensive deployment on the hill
• Abdullah ibn Jubayr clearly identifiable among them
• no hidden removal of archers
• no reduction from 50 in this scene

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"عبد الله بن جبير و الرماة"

STYLE

museum-quality sculpture realism
photoreal facial anatomy
realistic carved eyes, noses, lips, and beards
realistic stone drapery folds
no cartoon proportions
no stylized faces
hyper detailed sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

Direct sword-to-sword clash between Muslim and Quraysh fighters. Cavalry may appear at the edges. Keep the clash direct and immediate.

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Muslim fighters advancing.

Quraysh fighters retreating.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — BATTLEFIELD AT UHUD

Mount Uhud must appear in this scene inside the palace hall, because this scene belongs to the battle zone or direct Uhud positioning.
The mountain should remain behind the battlefield in the same visual logic used across the battle scenes.

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.

Muslim fighters push forward. Quraysh retreats backward. Keep the directionality clear and exact.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.

Keep the main duel clearly readable in front.

Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

EXTREME PHOTOREALISTIC HISTORICAL STONE SCULPTURE SCENE

ABSOLUTE VISUAL LOCK — DO NOT CHANGE THESE ELEMENTS

THE PALACE, THE ARCHITECTURAL PRESENTATION STYLE, THE CAMERA PRESENTATION LOGIC, THE LIGHTING MOOD, THE OPEN CENTRAL PRESENTATION SPACE, AND THE OVERALL DISPLAY METHOD MUST REMAIN UNIFIED IN ALL SCENES.
ONLY THE EVENT, CHARACTER ACTION, THE EXACT SCENARIO POSE, AND THE SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL FOCUS MAY CHANGE.

ENVIRONMENT

The scene takes place inside a vast monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall must appear extremely wide and open.

The central presentation area must remain completely open with no columns in the middle.

All square Islamic columns are placed along the outer palace walls only.

The columns are extremely tall and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque engravings.

Large arches connect the columns along the walls.

Warm golden sunlight enters from high palace openings creating cinematic light rays and floating dust particles.

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles surrounding the main dramatic area.

CRITICAL GROUND RULE

The central action area under all statues MUST be a raised desert sand platform.
All characters stand on this elevated sandy battlefield podium inside the palace.
Do NOT place characters directly on tiles.
The surrounding palace floor must remain polished Islamic ceramic tile.
No sand is allowed outside the raised platform.

CEILING LOCK

The palace ceiling must remain visible and consistent in all scenes.
It must include high Islamic architectural ceiling design with depth, arches, and upper openings.
Do not remove or simplify the ceiling in any scene.

CONSISTENCY RULE

The palace must remain the same palace in all scenes.
The architectural display style must remain identical in all scenes.
The presentation method must remain unified in all scenes.
Do not redesign the hall from one scene to another.
Do not change the placement logic of columns, arches, floor, or light.
Keep the same monumental museum-style presentation in every image.

SCULPTURE STYLE — EXTREME REALISM

All characters must appear as hyper-realistic carved sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality historical sculptures.

Stone surfaces must show:
fine sandstone grain
visible chisel marks
weathered edges
deep carved fabric folds

No cartoon style
No stylized art

FIXED COSTUME RULE

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

Khalid ibn al-Walid in full cavalry armor on horseback.

Cavalry with armor, spears, horses.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — BATTLEFIELD AT UHUD

Mount Uhud must appear in this scene inside the palace hall, because this scene belongs to the battle zone or direct Uhud positioning.
The mountain should remain behind the battlefield in the same visual logic used across the battle scenes.

STRICT POSE LOCK

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

Khalid ibn al-Walid leads the flanking movement from the rear. Keep him mounted and clearly leading the cavalry around from behind.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL FLANK LOCK

Khalid appears with a concentrated cavalry movement, not an entire new army.
Show a strong mounted flanking contingent with clear speed and discipline.
Do NOT overcrowd the frame with random extra horsemen.

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"حمزة بن عبد المطلب"

museum-quality sculpture realism
photoreal facial anatomy
realistic carved eyes, noses, lips, and beards
realistic stone drapery folds
no cartoon proportions
no stylized faces

photorealistic desert sand textures

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.

• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.

• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.
