• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

EXTREME PHOTOREALISTIC HISTORICAL STONE SCULPTURE SCENE

ABSOLUTE VISUAL LOCK — DO NOT CHANGE THESE ELEMENTS

THE PALACE, THE ARCHITECTURAL PRESENTATION STYLE, THE CAMERA PRESENTATION LOGIC, THE LIGHTING MOOD, THE OPEN CENTRAL PRESENTATION SPACE, AND THE OVERALL DISPLAY METHOD MUST REMAIN UNIFIED IN ALL SCENES.
ONLY THE EVENT, CHARACTER ACTION, THE EXACT SCENARIO POSE, AND THE SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL FOCUS MAY CHANGE.

ENVIRONMENT

The scene takes place inside a vast monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall must appear extremely wide and open.

The central presentation area must remain completely open with no columns in the middle.

All square Islamic columns are placed along the outer palace walls only.

The columns are extremely tall and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque engravings.

Large arches connect the columns along the walls.

Warm golden sunlight enters from high palace openings creating cinematic light rays and floating dust particles.

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles surrounding the main dramatic area.

CRITICAL GROUND RULE

The central action area under all statues MUST be a raised desert sand platform.
All characters stand on this elevated sandy battlefield podium inside the palace.
Do NOT place characters directly on tiles.
The surrounding palace floor must remain polished Islamic ceramic tile.
No sand is allowed outside the raised platform.

CEILING LOCK

The palace ceiling must remain visible and consistent in all scenes.
It must include high Islamic architectural ceiling design with depth, arches, and upper openings.
Do not remove or simplify the ceiling in any scene.

CONSISTENCY RULE

The palace must remain the same palace in all scenes.
The architectural display style must remain identical in all scenes.
The presentation method must remain unified in all scenes.
Do not redesign the hall from one scene to another.
Do not change the placement logic of columns, arches, floor, or light.
Keep the same monumental museum-style presentation in every image.

SCULPTURE STYLE — EXTREME REALISM

All characters must appear as hyper-realistic carved sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality historical sculptures.

Stone surfaces must show:
fine sandstone grain
visible chisel marks
weathered edges
deep carved fabric folds

No cartoon style
No stylized art

FIXED COSTUME RULE

Costume must follow the exact logic of this new scenario only.
Civilian and female non-combat scenes may use simple traditional Arabic garments without armor.
Combat scenes must use simple primitive Arab wrapped garments, straight Arab swords, spears, and plain round shields only when the scenario requires them.
No leather armor, no metal armor, no helmets, and no decorative weapons in any combat scene.

CHARACTER DETAILS

A Quraysh man shouting.

Muslim fighters reacting in shock.

BACKGROUND CONTEXT — BATTLEFIELD AT UHUD

Mount Uhud must appear in this scene inside the palace hall, because this scene belongs to the battle zone or direct Uhud positioning.
The mountain should remain behind the battlefield in the same visual logic used across the battle scenes.

STRICT POSE LOCK

You must follow the exact pose and body logic described below.
Do not reinterpret the gesture.
Do not replace the stance with a different dramatic pose.
Do not change the relative arrangement of the figures if the scenario specified a clear arrangement.

SCENE

A shout is released. Some Muslim fighters drop their weapons. Keep the shock reaction exact and visible.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

The original archer unit was 50.
In this scene:
• EXACTLY 40 archers are shown leaving the hill or moving downward
• EXACTLY 10 remain on the hill with Abdullah ibn Jubayr

Both parts must be visually understandable at the same time.
Do NOT randomize the split.
Do NOT leave the hill with zero defenders in this scene.

LIGHTING

Warm cinematic sunlight illuminates the central figures.
Light rays pass through the palace hall.
Dust particles float in the air creating dramatic historical atmosphere.

SANDY ROCK TEXT SURFACE

The text MUST match the scene definition exactly with no extra words and no old wording.
At the front of the scene a natural rough sandy sandstone rock surface shows flat handwritten ink text that reads:
The text must be written with normal ink or paint on rough desert sandstone, NOT on marble, NOT on polished stone, and it must stay completely flat on the surface, NOT carved, NOT engraved, NOT embossed, and NOT chiseled into the rock.
"الرماة"

photoreal facial anatomy
realistic carved eyes, noses, lips, and beards
realistic stone drapery folds
no cartoon proportions
no stylized faces

photorealistic desert sand textures

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:

wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates

straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

Ultra cinematic historical scene inside a monumental Islamic palace hall inspired by classical Middle Eastern architecture.

Image format:
16:9 cinematic aspect ratio
wide cinematic frame

Architecture:
a massive grand Islamic palace hall
very tall square stone columns covered entirely with deeply carved Islamic geometric patterns and arabesque engravings
intricate Islamic relief carvings clearly visible across the entire column surfaces
large traditional Islamic arches connecting the columns
a visible high palace ceiling with depth, upper openings, and clear architectural continuity
warm sunlight rays entering from high palace openings

Floor:
highly detailed Islamic ceramic tile flooring with geometric patterns
polished reflective ceramic surface around the main terrain only

Center structure:
in the center of the palace floor there is a natural raised rock island emerging from the ground
the island looks like a real piece of desert terrain placed inside the palace

Island terrain:
real desert sand terrain
loose wind-formed sand ripples
natural scattered sand across rough rock
uneven rocky edges blending naturally with the palace floor

Critical ground rule:
all statues must stand on sand or natural rough terrain only
no characters standing directly on the ceramic tiles

Statue realism:
museum-quality stone sculptures
extremely realistic sculpted facial anatomy
fine sandstone grain
subtle stone surface roughness
visible chisel marks following the form
weathered stone edges
realistic carved cloth folds
lifelike human proportions
NO smooth CGI surfaces
NO plastic look
NO stylized art
NO cartoon style

Lighting:
warm golden sunlight entering through palace openings
soft floating dust particles in the air
dramatic cinematic lighting

Visual contrast:
strong contrast between the polished Islamic ceramic palace floor and the natural desert sand island

Consistency rules:
the palace must remain the same palace in all scenes
the same ceiling must remain visible in all scenes
the same architectural presentation style must remain unified
the same lighting direction and visual mood must remain unified

Scene title:
Receiving the Sword — Abu Dujana

Main character — Abu Dujana:
a life-size hyper-realistic sandstone statue of a powerful Arab warrior
strong masculine facial structure
confident intense expression
broad shoulders
clear heroic presence

Clothing:
traditional early Arabian combat garments carved in stone
wrapped head cloth with a distinctive red headband translated into carved stone form
long robe
cloth belt
simple battlefield gear
no unnecessary decorative details

Weapon:
a sword held firmly in one hand

Pose:
Abu Dujana stands at the center of the desert island
one hand fixing or tightening the red headband around his head
the other hand holding the sword firmly
his body leans slightly forward with readiness and confidence
feet planted strongly in the sand
he must dominate the entire composition

Supporting figures:
a small group of warrior statues positioned behind him
they must remain secondary and visually pushed back
they must not compete with Abu Dujana for attention

Environmental feeling:
the scene must feel like a heroic pre-combat moment
the palace remains monumental and unchanged
the sand island remains the focal terrain

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"أبو دجانة – لحظة أخذ السيف عربي مستقيم"

Camera:
heroic medium-wide cinematic frame
front-centered composition
Abu Dujana must dominate the frame

Style:
ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting

They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Ultra cinematic historical scene inside a monumental Islamic palace hall inspired by classical Middle Eastern architecture.

Image format:
16:9 cinematic aspect ratio
wide cinematic frame

Architecture:
a massive grand Islamic palace hall
very tall square stone columns covered entirely with deeply carved Islamic geometric patterns and arabesque engravings
intricate Islamic relief carvings clearly visible across the entire column surfaces
large traditional Islamic arches connecting the columns
a visible high palace ceiling with depth, upper openings, and clear architectural continuity
warm sunlight rays entering from high palace openings

Floor:
highly detailed Islamic ceramic tile flooring with geometric patterns
polished reflective ceramic surface around the main terrain only

Center structure:
in the center of the palace floor there is a natural raised rock island emerging from the ground
the island looks like a real piece of desert terrain placed inside the palace

Island terrain:
real desert sand terrain
loose wind-formed sand ripples
natural scattered sand across rough rock
uneven rocky edges blending naturally with the palace floor

Critical ground rule:
all statues must stand on sand or natural rough terrain only
no characters standing directly on the ceramic tiles

Statue realism:
museum-quality stone sculptures
extremely realistic sculpted facial anatomy
fine sandstone grain
subtle stone surface roughness
visible chisel marks following the form
weathered stone edges
realistic carved cloth folds
lifelike human proportions
NO smooth CGI surfaces
NO plastic look
NO stylized art
NO cartoon style

Lighting:
warm golden sunlight entering through palace openings
soft floating dust particles in the air
dramatic cinematic lighting

Visual contrast:
strong contrast between the polished Islamic ceramic palace floor and the natural desert sand island

Consistency rules:
the palace must remain the same palace in all scenes
the same ceiling must remain visible in all scenes
the same architectural presentation style must remain unified
the same lighting direction and visual mood must remain unified

Scene title:
The First Duels

Main composition:
a direct early duel at the front of the battlefield
one Muslim warrior attacks with force
a Quraysh flag bearer begins to fall
the banner tilts visibly during the impact

Important focus rule:
the duel must remain the dominant subject
do not overcrowd the frame
background fighters must stay secondary and reduced in visual importance

Character details:
two main warrior statues in close combat
strong Arab facial features
battle intensity in the posture
clear difference between attacker and the falling opponent

Clothing:
early Arabian combat garments
plain leather or simple metal armor appropriate to the scenario
swords
shields
no decorative fantasy details

Pose:
the attacker lunges forward with aggressive force
the opponent loses balance and begins falling
the flag leans diagonally and visually signals the breaking of Quraysh morale
the whole scene must capture the instant of decisive first impact

Background:
a few secondary fighters only
enough to imply the opening of battle without stealing the focus from the duel

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"المبارزات الأولى – كسر هيبة قريش"

Camera:
dynamic action-focused cinematic frame
medium-wide composition
the two main fighters dominate the visual center

Style:
ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting
8K historical cinematic render

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

This is a focused first-duel composition.
Keep the duel readable with only light supporting presence in the background.
المشهد 28 Prompt كامل — Ultra Strict

مصعب بن عمير واللواء

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

Banner identity and face must remain fixed

no age drift
no identity change

Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.

Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.

No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.

No cel-shading.

The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.

The Fall of the Banner — Musab ibn Umayr

Main composition:
Musab ibn Umayr at the last upright moment before collapse
the banner slipping from his hand
enemy pressure closing in around him

Pose:
Musab remains upright for one final symbolic moment
the banner falls diagonally from his grasp
his body carries the tension of imminent martyrdom
the emotional focus must be on the banner, the hand, and the final stillness before collapse

Background:
enemy figures appear close but must not dominate the frame
the central emotional subject is the falling banner

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"مصعب بن عمير – سقوط اللواء"

Camera:
dramatic falling-banner cinematic frame
medium composition focused on the upper body, hand, and flag movement

ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting

The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

Ultra cinematic historical scene inside a monumental Islamic palace hall inspired by classical Middle Eastern architecture.

Image format:
16:9 cinematic aspect ratio
wide cinematic frame

Architecture:
a massive grand Islamic palace hall
very tall square stone columns covered entirely with deeply carved Islamic geometric patterns and arabesque engravings
intricate Islamic relief carvings clearly visible across the entire column surfaces
large traditional Islamic arches connecting the columns
a visible high palace ceiling with depth, upper openings, and clear architectural continuity
warm sunlight rays entering from high palace openings

Floor:
highly detailed Islamic ceramic tile flooring with geometric patterns
polished reflective ceramic surface around the main terrain only

Center structure:
in the center of the palace floor there is a natural raised rock island emerging from the ground
the island looks like a real piece of desert terrain placed inside the palace

Island terrain:
real desert sand terrain
loose wind-formed sand ripples
natural scattered sand across rough rock
uneven rocky edges blending naturally with the palace floor

Mount Uhud:
behind the battlefield stands a massive Mount Uhud rock formation inside the palace hall
the mountain must be extremely large with layered desert rock cliffs and sand dunes at the base
the mountain must feel monumental, never miniature

Critical ground rule:
all statues must stand on sand or natural rough terrain only
no characters standing directly on the ceramic tiles

Statue realism:
museum-quality stone sculptures
extremely realistic sculpted facial anatomy
fine sandstone grain
subtle stone surface roughness
visible chisel marks following the form
weathered stone edges
realistic carved cloth folds
lifelike human proportions
NO smooth CGI surfaces
NO plastic look
NO stylized art
NO cartoon style

Lighting:
warm golden sunlight entering through palace openings
soft floating dust particles in the air
dramatic cinematic lighting

Visual contrast:
strong contrast between the polished Islamic ceramic palace floor and the natural desert sand island

Consistency rules:
the palace must remain the same palace in all scenes
the same ceiling must remain visible in all scenes
the same architectural presentation style must remain unified
the same lighting direction and visual mood must remain unified

Scene title:
Hamza Before Martyrdom

Main character — Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib:
a life-size hyper-realistic sandstone statue of a towering powerful Arab warrior
broad shoulders
strong chest
dominant battlefield presence
severe intense facial expression
heroic body scale within normal human proportions

Clothing:
traditional Arabian combat garments carved in stone
battle sword
plain combat details appropriate to the scenario
optional subtle visual reference to the known battle marker on his chest if kept within the established style

Pose:
Hamza pushes forward through enemy lines
his body leans into the attack
the sword cuts open a path through surrounding enemies
enemy bodies visually recoil around him
Hamza must dominate the frame as the strongest force in motion

Background:
enemy fighters remain secondary to his breakthrough
the mountain and palace must stay consistent with the established battle scenes

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"حمزة بن عبد المطلب – ذروة القتال"

Camera:
heroic breakthrough cinematic frame
medium-wide composition
Hamza dominates the visual center

Style:
ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting
8K historical cinematic render

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

Hamzah must dominate the frame against a limited visible cluster of Quraysh fighters.
He is breaking through a local group, not standing alone in an empty space and not lost in a giant crowd.

استشهاد حمزة

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

Jawline: sharp and defined
Eyes: deep-set focused eyes
Eyebrows: thick natural brows
Nose: straight strong bridge
Facial hair: short controlled beard only
Skin: young, smooth, no wrinkles
Head size: natural proportion

He must remain clearly young

STRICT NEGATIVE
no old face
no wrinkles
no heavy beard redesign
no identity change

Jawline: heavy and strong

Skin: mature with very limited age lines only

no frail old-man look
no beard redesign
no face variation
no identity change

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Ultra cinematic historical scene inside a monumental Islamic palace hall inspired by classical Middle Eastern architecture.

Image format:
16:9 cinematic aspect ratio
wide cinematic frame

Architecture:
a massive grand Islamic palace hall
very tall square stone columns covered entirely with deeply carved Islamic geometric patterns and arabesque engravings
intricate Islamic relief carvings clearly visible across the entire column surfaces
large traditional Islamic arches connecting the columns
a visible high palace ceiling with depth, upper openings, and clear architectural continuity
warm sunlight rays entering from high palace openings

Floor:
highly detailed Islamic ceramic tile flooring with geometric patterns
polished reflective ceramic surface around the main terrain only

Center structure:
in the center of the palace floor there is a natural raised rock island emerging from the ground
the island looks like a real piece of desert terrain placed inside the palace

Island terrain:
real desert sand terrain
loose wind-formed sand ripples
natural scattered sand across rough rock
uneven rocky edges blending naturally with the palace floor

Critical ground rule:
all statues must stand on sand or natural rough terrain only
no characters standing directly on the ceramic tiles

Statue realism:
museum-quality stone sculptures
extremely realistic sculpted facial anatomy
fine sandstone grain
subtle stone surface roughness
visible chisel marks following the form
weathered stone edges
realistic carved cloth folds
lifelike human proportions
NO smooth CGI surfaces
NO plastic look
NO stylized art
NO cartoon style

Lighting:
warm golden sunlight entering through palace openings
soft floating dust particles in the air
gentle dramatic cinematic lighting

Visual contrast:
strong contrast between the polished Islamic ceramic palace floor and the natural desert sand island

Consistency rules:
the palace must remain the same palace in all scenes
the same ceiling must remain visible in all scenes
the same architectural presentation style must remain unified
the same lighting direction and visual mood must remain unified

Scene title:
Hanzala

Main composition:
the body of Hanzala lies extended on the sand in complete stillness

Pose:
full-body stillness
no violent motion
solemn calm after martyrdom
the scene must feel sacred and quiet rather than active or chaotic

Symbolic tone:
a restrained upper light or subtle spiritual visual cue may appear
do not exaggerate
do not introduce fantasy wings or cartoon symbolism
keep the tone elegant, quiet, and reverent

Background:
minimize surrounding figures
keep the scene open and calm
the palace remains the same palace, but the emotional energy is transformed into stillness

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"حنظلة – غسيل الملائكة"

Camera:
quiet stillness cinematic frame
calm medium-wide composition with strong silence and negative space

Style:
ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting
8K historical cinematic render

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

This is a solitary, quiet scene for Hanzalah.
Do NOT crowd the frame with extra figures.

نهاية المعركة

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.

Scene title:
The End of the Battle

Main composition:
the battle has ended
fighters stand, sit, or lean in exhaustion
weapons are lowered
no active combat remains

Pose:
some figures remain upright in silence
some sit or lean with fatigue
the whole composition must communicate emotional heaviness, not renewed motion

Background:
the battlefield remains present in traces only
no fresh action
no dramatic attack gestures
this is a silence-after-chaos scene

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"نهاية المعركة – الصمت بعد الصدام"

Camera:
aftermath wide cinematic frame
readable group composition with strong emotional stillness

Style:
ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting
8K historical cinematic render

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.

HISTORICAL AFTERMATH LOCK

This is the aftermath of battle.
Show a broad sense of loss and scattered battlefield exhaustion.
Do NOT accidentally return the armies to full organized battle formation.

Scene title:
Burial of the Martyrs

Main composition:
a small group of men lowers the bodies into sand graves
other men stand nearby in silence and reverence

Pose:
slow respectful movement
heads lowered
arms carefully supporting the bodies
no battle gestures
no aggressive motion
the entire scene must feel like a solemn farewell

Background:
keep the environment calm and minimal
the palace remains unchanged
the emotional focus is burial, dignity, and grief

Stone plaque:
in front of the desert island place a carved stone plaque engraved with Arabic text:

"دفن شهداء أحد"

Camera:
farewell burial cinematic frame
medium-wide respectful composition with strong human dignity

Style:
ultra detailed environment
hyper-realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic materials
cinematic lighting
8K historical cinematic render

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL BURIAL LOCK

The martyrs of Uhud were many.
The burial scene must suggest a large number of martyrs and a heavy communal loss.
Use grouped burial logic and repeated wrapped forms or graves to imply many martyrs without needing every single body individually isolated.
Do NOT show only one or two burials.
غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · التكتيك
صفحة 1

التكتيك

هذا القسم يشرح المنطق العسكري للمشاهد الأساسية داخل المعركة، وربطه بصريًا بالموقع والجبل والرماة والالتفاف.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · التكتيك
صفحة 2
تكتيك Battle Logic

الخطة العامة

الشرح

التمركز عند جبل أُحد يجعل الجبل حاجزًا خلفيًا طبيعيًا، ويمنح الجيش خطًا دفاعيًا واضحًا داخل ساحة مفتوحة. هذا يفسر أهمية اختيار الموضع قبل بدء القتال.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · التكتيك
صفحة 3
تكتيك Battle Logic

جبل الرماة

الشرح

وجود الرماة على عينين هو عنصر حماية حاسم لمنع الالتفاف من الخلف. سقوط هذا الموقع هو نقطة التحول الأكبر في السيناريو.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · التكتيك
صفحة 4
تكتيك Battle Logic

التفاف خالد

الشرح

عندما نزل أكثر الرماة، ظهر الفراغ التكتيكي الذي استغله خالد بن الوليد بفرسانه عبر مسار التفاف جانبي ثم خلفي.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · التكتيك
صفحة 5
تكتيك Battle Logic

الكماشة

الشرح

بعد سقوط موقع الرماة، أصبح الضغط من الأمام والخلف في وقت واحد، وهو ما يحوّل المشهد من معركة تقليدية إلى اختناق قتالي داخل الوسط.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · Camera Prompts
صفحة 1

Camera Prompts

هذه البرومبتات تغيّر الكاميرا فقط ولا تغيّر أي عنصر في المشهد. كل برومبت يؤكد على الحفاظ على نفس القصر، نفس السقف، نفس الأرض الرملية، نفس الإضاءة، نفس الشخصيات، ونفس الوضعية الأصلية. وهي تشمل كلوزات على الشخصيات المهمة، بالإضافة إلى لونج وايد، درون، وأوربيت.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · Camera Prompts
صفحة 2
المشهد 2 Camera Prompts

خالد بن الوليد — مجموعة زوايا

Close Portrait

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

CHARACTER: Khalid ibn al-Walid

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Young-to-mature warrior (30–35)
Athletic mounted commander build
Mounted presence, fast tactical energy
Simple Arab wrapped garments, plain head wrap, spear, straight Arab sword

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: strong athletic Arab face
Jawline: defined
Eyes: sharp tactical gaze
Beard: shorter controlled beard
Skin: active mature skin, no aging exaggeration

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same mounted commander across all scenes
Horse gear, armor, and face must stay consistent

STRICT NEGATIVE
no beard redesign
no different horse logic
no identity change

SCENE-TO-SCENE CONSISTENCY LOCK
This is the same recurring character across all related scenes. Keep identity, age, face, beard, clothing logic, and body proportions fixed.



STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock, the same palace, the same ceiling, the same sandy battlefield, the same clothing logic, the same lighting, and the same statue identity. Move the camera only for a tight close portrait of Khalid ibn al-Walid on horseback. Keep his exact facial features, armor, spear, horse, and expression unchanged. Do not add or remove any figures. Do not redesign the palace. Focus tightly on Khalid from chest-up or horse-saddle-up while preserving the same background logic and warm cinematic light.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

This is a two-person negotiation scene.
Keep Hind bint Utbah and Wahshi as the dominant readable figures.
Do NOT add extra negotiators or an unnecessary crowd.

long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and the exact same scene. Change only the camera to an orbit three-quarter angle around Khalid ibn al-Walid. Keep all statues, horses, spacing, armor, and the palace architecture identical. Only the camera moves.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

This is a two-person negotiation scene.
Keep Hind bint Utbah and Wahshi as the dominant readable figures.
Do NOT add extra negotiators or an unnecessary crowd.
غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · Camera Prompts
صفحة 3
المشهد 10 Camera Prompts

عبد الله بن أبي — مجموعة زوايا

Close Portrait

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same scene. Only move the camera for a close dramatic portrait of Abdullah ibn Ubayy in his 3/4 withdrawing pose. Keep his exact expression of objection and pride, his light armor, side sword, lowered shield, and the followers behind him unchanged. No redesign, no pose change.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

Abdullah ibn Ubayy withdraws with about 300 men.
This withdrawing block must feel substantial and clearly separated from the main Muslim force.

Scene rules:
• front: Abdullah ibn Ubayy leading the withdrawal
• behind him: a visibly large attached withdrawing contingent
• in the far background: the remaining Muslim force still holding position

Do NOT confuse the withdrawing 300 with the whole army.
