Abdullah ibn Ubayy withdraws with about 300 men.
This withdrawing block must feel substantial and clearly separated from the main Muslim force.

Scene rules:
• front: Abdullah ibn Ubayy leading the withdrawal
• behind him: a visibly large attached withdrawing contingent
• in the far background: the remaining Muslim force still holding position

Do NOT confuse the withdrawing 300 with the whole army.
غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · Camera Prompts
صفحة 4
المشهد 14 Camera Prompts

عبد الله بن جبير والرماة — مجموعة زوايا

Close Portrait

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and the exact same scene. Move the camera only for a close portrait of Abdullah ibn Jubayr on the archer hill. Keep his exact bow, armor, stance, and focused expression unchanged. Do not alter the archers behind him or the hill geometry.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL COUNT LOCK

EXACTLY 50 archers on the hill under Abdullah ibn Jubayr.
No more, no less.
All 50 must be visibly readable as a defended archer position.

Position rules:
• one continuous defensive deployment on the hill
• Abdullah ibn Jubayr clearly identifiable among them
• no hidden removal of archers
• no reduction from 50 in this scene

• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

CHARACTER: Khalid ibn al-Walid

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Young-to-mature warrior (30–35)
Athletic mounted commander build
Mounted presence, fast tactical energy
Simple Arab wrapped garments, plain head wrap, spear, straight Arab sword

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: strong athletic Arab face
Jawline: defined
Eyes: sharp tactical gaze
Beard: shorter controlled beard
Skin: active mature skin, no aging exaggeration

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same mounted commander across all scenes
Horse gear, armor, and face must stay consistent

STRICT NEGATIVE
no beard redesign
no different horse logic
no identity change

SCENE-TO-SCENE CONSISTENCY LOCK
This is the same recurring character across all related scenes. Keep identity, age, face, beard, clothing logic, and body proportions fixed.



STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same flanking scene. Move the camera only for a close heroic portrait of Khalid ibn al-Walid mounted on horseback while leading the flanking cavalry. Preserve all armor, spear, horse details, and facial identity exactly.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL FLANK LOCK

Khalid appears with a concentrated cavalry movement, not an entire new army.
Show a strong mounted flanking contingent with clear speed and discipline.
Do NOT overcrowd the frame with random extra horsemen.

• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same scene. Move only the camera for a tight close portrait of Saad ibn Abi Waqqas at full draw. Keep the exact bow tension, gaze line, armor, and arrow alignment unchanged.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

The rumor is triggered by one Quraysh caller in the foreground and a limited Muslim reaction group.
Keep the focus on the shout and the emotional shock.
Do NOT turn this into a giant crowd scene.

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same scene. Move only the camera for a dramatic close portrait of Umm Ammarah in active defense. Keep her sword, armor, facial expression, and the attacking Quraysh fighter unchanged.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

Keep the frame centered on Umm Ammarah in direct defense combat.
Any supporting fighters must remain secondary.
Do NOT dilute her by adding a large crowd.

Long Wide

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.

Long Wide

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition

إضافة

Camera Prompts للمشاهد المضافة

تمت إضافة زوايا كاميرا للمشاهد الجديدة والشخصيات الأهم فيها بنفس نظام الـ close / long wide / drone / orbit مع الحفاظ على كل عناصر اللوك الأصلي.

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

CHARACTER: Abu Dujana

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Mature warrior (30–40)
Strong athletic build, broad shoulders
Red headband, sword, direct heroic presence

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: rectangular to strong oval
Jawline: strong
Eyes: intense brave eyes
Expression: heroic confidence
Beard: controlled consistent beard

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same warrior across all scenes
Red headband and sword identity must remain fixed

STRICT NEGATIVE
no identity change
no beard drift
no headband removal
no age shift

SCENE-TO-SCENE CONSISTENCY LOCK
This is the same recurring character across all related scenes. Keep identity, age, face, beard, clothing logic, and body proportions fixed.



STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same scene. Only change the camera to a long wide shot that keeps Abu Dujana dominant while showing a little more of the desert island and the palace hall.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

This is a focused hero moment for Abu Dujanah receiving and binding himself to the sword.
Keep nearby companions limited and readable.
Do NOT turn the moment into a mass crowd shot.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same scene. Only move the camera for a powerful close portrait of Hamza before martyrdom. Preserve the same body scale, sword, expression, and forceful forward posture.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL GROUPING LOCK

Hamzah must dominate the frame against a limited visible cluster of Quraysh fighters.
He is breaking through a local group, not standing alone in an empty space and not lost in a giant crowd.

Long Wide

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

CHARACTER: Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Mature powerful man (55–60)
Large muscular warrior build
Dominant force, strong chest and shoulders
Sword, heavy presence

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: wide powerful mature face
Jawline: heavy and strong
Eyes: intense commanding eyes
Eyebrows: strong
Beard: full controlled beard
Skin: mature with very limited age lines only

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same warrior across all scenes
He must remain powerful, not fragile, not elderly-weak

STRICT NEGATIVE
no frail old-man look
no beard redesign
no face variation
no identity change

SCENE-TO-SCENE CONSISTENCY LOCK
This is the same recurring character across all related scenes. Keep identity, age, face, beard, clothing logic, and body proportions fixed.



STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.

Long Wide

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

CHARACTER: Wahshi ibn Harb

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Young man (20–35, visually young)
Abyssinian origin
Lean athletic body
Simple clothing, long spear, no heavy armor

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: oval to slightly rectangular
Jawline: sharp and defined
Eyes: deep-set focused eyes
Eyebrows: thick natural brows
Nose: straight strong bridge
Facial hair: short controlled beard only
Skin: young, smooth, no wrinkles
Head size: natural proportion

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same recurring character across all scenes
He must remain clearly young

STRICT NEGATIVE
no old face
no wrinkles
no heavy beard redesign
no identity change

CHARACTER: Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib

MASTER CHARACTER IDENTITY — LOCKED
Mature powerful man (55–60)
Large muscular warrior build
Dominant force, strong chest and shoulders
Sword, heavy presence

FACE DNA — EXACT MATCH REQUIRED
Face shape: wide powerful mature face
Jawline: heavy and strong
Eyes: intense commanding eyes
Eyebrows: strong
Beard: full controlled beard
Skin: mature with very limited age lines only

CONSISTENCY LOCK
Same warrior across all scenes
He must remain powerful, not fragile, not elderly-weak

STRICT NEGATIVE
no frail old-man look
no beard redesign
no face variation
no identity change

SCENE-TO-SCENE CONSISTENCY LOCK
This is the same recurring character across all related scenes. Keep identity, age, face, beard, clothing logic, and body proportions fixed.



STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.
No exaggerated expressions.
No stylized anatomy.
No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.

No toy-like forms.
No soft or childish facial design.
No CGI look.
No cel-shading.
No fake glow.
No oversaturated colors.
The result must feel realistic, cinematic, and historically believable.

ENVIRONMENT CONSISTENCY:
The palace must feel like the exact same palace in every scene.
The architectural presentation style must remain unified in all scenes.
The lighting direction, dust atmosphere, scale, and dramatic mood must remain consistent.
If the original scene is a battlefield scene, keep Mount Uhud or battlefield geography when required by that scene.
If the original scene is not a battlefield-wide shot, keep the same palace lighting and realism without forcing extra battle elements that are not mentioned in the original scene prompt.

STATUE RULE:
All characters must appear as hyper-realistic sandstone statues.
They must look like museum-quality carved stone sculptures with detailed stone texture.
Faces, anatomy, hands, eyes, noses, lips, beards, and cloth folds must be highly realistic and historically believable as carved stone, not simplified or cartoon-like.
No living humans.
No glossy marble.
No cartoon stylization.
No toy-like proportions.
No exaggerated facial features.
No soft animation look.
No simplified sculpting.
Facial structure must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply carved like real museum statues.

CLOTHING RULE — DO NOT CHANGE:
Early Arabian clothing:
wrapped desert turbans
cloth wrapped around the neck and shoulders
long loose Arabian robes
wide cloth belts around the waist
layered cloth across the chest

ARMOR RULE:
simple wrapped cloth protection only
no leather armor
no metal chest plates
No fantasy armor.

WEAPONS RULE:
straight early Arabian swords
long spears
plain round shields
traditional bows only when clearly required by the scene

LIGHTING RULE:
Strong golden sunlight rays with realistic shadow contrast.
Visible dust in the air.
Cinematic historical atmosphere.
Photorealistic stone and sand textures.

REALISM STYLE:
ultra detailed environment
hyper realistic sandstone statues
photorealistic desert sand textures
cinematic historical lighting
epic dramatic composition
8K cinematic render

CRITICAL:
Preserve the exact scene-specific event, character identities, pose logic, and composition instructions written below.
Only unify the environment, lighting, realism level, and material quality according to this visual lock.

Use the exact same visual lock and exact same scene. Only move the camera closer to the hands and bodies during burial while preserving all pose logic and solemn mood.

HISTORICAL ACCURACY LOCK

Follow only the historically established event logic of Uhud in this scene.
Do NOT invent extra troops, extra commanders, extra weapons, or extra formations.
Do NOT swap sides.
Do NOT increase or decrease any historically important small group.
If a large army is described, it must feel large through density and layered formations, not as a small scattered crowd.

HISTORICAL BURIAL LOCK

The martyrs of Uhud were many.
The burial scene must suggest a large number of martyrs and a heavy communal loss.
Use grouped burial logic and repeated wrapped forms or graves to imply many martyrs without needing every single body individually isolated.
Do NOT show only one or two burials.

Long Wide

GLOBAL UHUD VISUAL LOCK — APPLY TO THIS SCENE EXACTLY

16:9 cinematic frame

CAMERA FRAMING FIX — STRICT:

• The camera must be positioned further back (step backward) to create a wider composition.
• The full battlefield area MUST be visible from foreground to midground.
• The foreground sand area with the rock must occupy at least the lower third of the frame.
• The rock with text MUST appear clearly inside the frame, not cropped and not touching the bottom edge.
• Maintain full visual breathing space around the rock with a clear margin from all frame edges.

• DO NOT zoom in.
• DO NOT crop the foreground.
• DO NOT push the rock outside the frame.
• Use a slightly wider lens equivalent to 18mm–24mm while maintaining believable perspective.
• Camera height remains eye-level around 1.6m–1.7m, but the camera position is pulled backward.

BATTLEFIELD PLATFORM & TEXT ROCK — FINAL POSITIONING (STRICT):

The battlefield is an elevated sand platform (podium) where the statues stand.

TEXT ROCK POSITION (CRITICAL):
• The rock with the text is placed EXACTLY at the FRONT EDGE of the elevated sand platform.
• The rock is touching the boundary of the platform, not separated from it.
• The rock sits at the transition between the palace ceramic floor and the sand platform.
• The rock is NOT deep in the foreground and NOT buried inside the sand area.
• The rock must remain fully visible and clearly readable.

PLATFORM STRUCTURE:
• The sand platform is raised and clearly higher than the palace floor.
• The platform is surrounded by rough natural sandstone rock edges.
• The statues stand ONLY on top of the elevated sand platform.
• The platform edges must feel natural, uneven, and organic, never geometric or perfectly shaped.

SAND BEHAVIOR — STRICT:
• The sand exists ONLY inside this raised battlefield platform.
• The sand must NOT spill outside the sandstone boundary.
• The sand surface contains realistic wind patterns, soft dunes, and natural desert texture.

PALACE FLOOR — IMPORTANT CHANGE:
• All surrounding ground outside the platform is polished Islamic ceramic tile.
• There must be NO sand on the palace floor outside the platform.
• The ceramic floor extends clearly across the hall around the platform.
• The tiles may show subtle reflections and traditional Islamic patterning.

DEPTH COMPOSITION — LOCKED:

1. Foreground → polished palace ceramic floor
2. Transition line → text rock placed exactly at the front edge of the platform
3. Mid-ground to background → elevated sand battlefield platform with statues, surrounded by sandstone rocks

STRICT:
• No sand outside the platform.
• The rock must sit exactly at the platform edge, not far forward and not deep inside the platform.
• The contrast between ceramic floor and sand platform must be very clear.
• The battlefield must read as a contained elevated podium of sand.
• The sandstone edge around the platform must remain visible.

STRICT ENVIRONMENT RULES — DO NOT CHANGE:
The scene takes place inside a monumental Islamic palace hall.
The hall contains extremely tall square stone columns only.
Columns must be square, not round, and covered with deep Islamic geometric carvings and arabesque patterns.
Large Islamic arches connect the columns.
Warm golden sunlight enters through tall palace windows creating cinematic light beams and dust particles in the air.
The palace floor is polished Islamic ceramic tiles across the hall.
The sandy dramatic area exists ONLY as a raised battlefield platform for the statues.
No sand is allowed on the palace floor outside that platform.
Keep the palace ceiling visible with monumental Islamic architectural depth.
Keep the same warm golden cinematic lighting mood in all scenes.
Keep the same realism quality in all scenes.
Do not turn the scene into illustration, concept art, cartoon, or stylized painting.

ULTRA REALISM LOCK:
The final image must look like a real cinematic photograph of museum-quality sandstone statues inside a monumental historical palace.
Use physically believable lighting, realistic material response, natural shadow falloff, subtle atmospheric depth, and true-to-life stone texture.
Faces, hands, cloth folds, and anatomy must be realistic, mature, proportionate, and sharply defined.
Keep the scene grounded, serious, historical, and photoreal.
Avoid any cartoon feeling, animation look, game render feel, concept-art style, painterly treatment, plastic texture, or fantasy exaggeration.

غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · Dialogue
إضافة

المحادثات المباشرة

هذا القسم يضم النصوص والعبارات المباشرة أو الأقرب للحوار، المرتبطة بالمشاهد التي فيها قول منسوب واضح لشخص بعينه أو نداء مباشر داخل الحدث.

المشهد 26 Dialogue

أبو دجانة

بينالنداء العام / أبو دجانة
نوع النصسيرة / قول منقول بالمعنى

النص

«من يأخذ هذا السيف عربي مستقيم بحقه؟» ثم تقدّم أبو دجانة وقال: «أنا آخذه بحقه».

المصدر

كتب السيرة والمغازي

ملاحظة

هذا هو الحوار الأكثر مباشرة المرتبط بمشهد أخذ السيف عربي مستقيم وبداية بروز أبي دجانة.

المشهد 31 Dialogue

وحشي وحمزة

بينوحشي / حمزة بن عبد المطلب
نوع النصحديث / رواية مباشرة

النص

في رواية وحشي: «فرميتُه بحربتي فوقعت فيه».

المصدر

صحيح البخاري

ملاحظة

هذه العبارة هي الأساس المباشر لبناء مشهد استشهاد حمزة كما في المشروع.

المشهد 32 Dialogue

أنس بن النضر

بينأنس بن النضر / من حوله في ساحة أُحد
نوع النصحديث صحيح

النص

«والله إني لأجد ريح الجنة دون أُحد».

المصدر

صحيح البخاري وصحيح مسلم بالمعنى المشهور

ملاحظة

هذا أقوى نص مباشر مرتبط بمشهد أنس بن النضر، ويعطيه طابعًا حواريًا واضحًا.

فهرس المشاهد

35 مشهد

تم دمج الخلفية داخل ملف HTML نفسه. هذه النسخة مبنية بالكامل على السيناريو المرفوع في الملف النصي الجديد.
غزوة أُحد - شهود الزمن · Global Prompt Lock
إضافة نهائية

GLOBAL PROMPT LOCK — FINAL

STRICT HISTORICAL ACCURACY — DO NOT BREAK

ALL soldiers and warriors must follow early Arab battlefield authenticity.

FORBIDDEN
- no leather armor
- no metal armor
- no helmets
- no European swords
- no curved fantasy swords
- no engravings on weapons
- no decorative armor

MANDATORY CLOTHING
- simple primitive wrapped Arab garments
- plain head wraps only
- rough simple sandals or barefoot when appropriate
- no ornamental costume drift

WEAPONS
- straight early Arab swords only
- plain spears
- plain round shields only when required

CROWD SCALE
- whenever the scenario mentions large numbers, render the force as visually many fighters filling depth and layers
- do not force a literal numeric count inside the frame
- the image must read as a large army at first glance

PRIORITY
If any scene wording conflicts with this block, this block wins.
STATUE MATERIAL — ULTRA STRICT: All characters must be made of REALISTIC NATURAL SANDSTONE ONLY. Material must be: • rough natural sandstone • visible stone grain • micro surface imperfections • subtle erosion marks • sharp chisel carving marks • small cracks and edge wear ABSOLUTE RULES: • NO clay material • NO mud texture • NO soft sculpting • NO smooth or plastic surface • NO wax or ceramic look • NO wet surface • NO rounded soft edges • NO blurred details The statues must feel: • hard • heavy • dry • carved from real desert rock Edges must be: • sharp where carved • slightly broken or chipped where aged Surface must react to light like real stone: • matte • no shine • no gloss • no reflection COLOR: • warm desert sandstone tones • natural beige / dusty brown variations • subtle color irregularity DETAIL LEVEL: • hyper realistic carving depth • deep fabric folds carved in stone • realistic facial anatomy carved sharply • pores NOT visible FINAL LOOK: museum-quality ancient sandstone statues photographed in real light If the result looks like clay, mud, wax, plastic, or soft sculpting → REJECT and regenerate.
